title: Users’ Expectations and Practices with Agent Memory
one_sentence_summary: Faculty of Information University of Toronto Toronto, Ontario, Canada brennan.jones@utoronto.ca
research_problem: “I can imagine, like, a scenario where, for example, I learned Haskell using GPT, and then I start a fresh chat to learn, let’s say, Swift.
method_summary: We conducted semi-structured one-on-one interviews (see Appendix A for the interview guide) with six participants (each identified as ‘P#’ in the findings) who use personalized AI tools with long-term memory on a regular basis.
main_findings: AI agents have the potential to provide long-term personalized assistance to users, and this relies on effective long-term memory.

section_map.heading: Users’ Expectations and Practices with Agent Memory
